title: Implicit function
body:
In mathematics, an implicit equation is a relation of the form 
  
    
      
        R
        (
        
          x
          
            1
          
        
        ,
        …
        ,
        
          x
          
            n
          
        
        )
        =
        0
        ,
      
    
    {\displaystyle R(x_{1},\dots ,x_{n})=0,}
  
 where R is a function of several variables (often a polynomial). For example, the implicit equation of the unit circle is 
  
    
      
        
          x
          
            2
          
        
        +
        
          y
          
            2
          
        
        −
        1
        =
        0.
      
    
    {\displaystyle x^{2}+y^{2}-1=0.}
  

An implicit function is a function that is defined by an implicit equation, that relates one of the variables, considered as the value of the function, with the others considered as the arguments. For example, the equation 
  
    
      
        
          x
          
            2
          
        
        +
        
          y
          
            2
          
        
        −
        1
        =
        0
      
    
    {\displaystyle x^{2}+y^{2}-1=0}
  
 of the unit circle defines y as an implicit function of x, 
  
    
      
        y
        =
        
          
            1
            −
            
              x
              
                2
              
            
          
        
      
    
    {\displaystyle y={\sqrt {1-x^{2}}}}
  
, assuming −1 ≤ x ≤ 1 and y is restricted to nonnegative values. 
Some equations do not admit an explicit solution.
The implicit function theorem provides conditions under which some kinds of implicit equations define implicit functions, namely those that are obtained by equating to zero multivariable functions that are continuously differentiable.
